title.en: Health Insurance for Undocumented Immigrants 2026 | CoveredUSA
title.es: Seguro Medico para Inmigrantes Indocumentados 2026 | CoveredUSA

description.en: State programs in CA, NY, IL, OR for undocumented immigrants 2026. Medi-Cal freeze, OHP, Emergency Medicaid, FQHCs, and no-ACA alternatives explained.
description.es: Programas estatales en CA, NY, IL, OR para inmigrantes indocumentados en 2026. Medi-Cal, OHP, Medicaid de emergencia y alternativas sin ACA explicadas.

h1.en: Health Insurance for Undocumented Immigrants in 2026
h1.es: Seguro Medico para Inmigrantes Indocumentados en 2026

subhero.en: Federal marketplace plans and Medicaid are off-limits for most undocumented immigrants, but four states have created real alternatives. Where you live determines whether you have full Medicaid, emergency-only coverage, or free clinic access in 2026.
subhero.es: Los planes del mercado federal y el Medicaid estan fuera del alcance de la mayoria de los inmigrantes no autorizados, pero cuatro estados han creado alternativas reales. Donde vive determina si tiene Medicaid completo, cobertura de emergencia solamente o acceso a clinicas gratuitas en 2026.

quickAnswer.en: Undocumented immigrants, unauthorized immigrants, and mixed-status families cannot buy ACA Marketplace plans or receive federal Premium Tax Credits in 2026, and most cannot enroll in federal Medicaid. The best option depends on the state: California froze new Medi-Cal enrollment for undocumented adults on January 1, 2026, though existing enrollees keep coverage; Oregon (OHP) covers all ages and immigration statuses at or below 138% of the 2026 Federal Poverty Level; New York covers undocumented seniors age 65 and older through state-funded Medicaid; and Illinois eliminated its immigrant adult program in July 2025, leaving Emergency Medicaid as the main fallback. For income-eligible undocumented immigrants in non-expansion states, Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) offer sliding-scale primary care regardless of immigration status. Noncitizen health coverage options in states without dedicated programs are limited but not zero. Emergency Medicaid and EMTALA hospital rights apply nationwide.
quickAnswer.es: Los inmigrantes indocumentados, inmigrantes no autorizados y familias de estatus mixto no pueden comprar planes del Mercado del ACA ni recibir Creditos Fiscales de Prima federales en 2026, y la mayoria no puede inscribirse en el Medicaid federal. La mejor opcion depende del estado: California congelo nuevas inscripciones en Medi-Cal para adultos indocumentados el 1 de enero de 2026, aunque los inscritos existentes mantienen su cobertura; Oregon (OHP) cubre todas las edades y estados migratorios con ingresos hasta el 138% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza de 2026; Nueva York cubre a los mayores de 65 anos indocumentados a traves del Medicaid financiado por el estado; e Illinois elimino su programa para adultos inmigrantes en julio de 2025, dejando al Medicaid de emergencia como principal alternativa. Los Centros de Salud Calificados Federalmente (FQHCs) ofrecen atencion primaria a escala variable independientemente del estatus migratorio.

introParagraphs.en: Undocumented immigrants and mixed-status families face a coverage landscape almost entirely shaped by state policy choices, not federal programs. Federal law bars unauthorized immigrants from enrolling in ACA Marketplace plans, receiving Premium Tax Credits, or joining standard Medicaid, with one significant federal exception: Emergency Medicaid (noncitizen health coverage for acute emergencies), which reimburses hospitals for stabilizing any patient regardless of immigration status under EMTALA. Beyond that floor, coverage in 2026 varies from near-universal (Oregon) to emergency-only (most states), depending entirely on where a person lives.
introParagraphs.es: Los inmigrantes indocumentados y las familias de estatus mixto enfrentan un panorama de cobertura moldeado casi por completo por las decisiones de politica estatal, no por programas federales. La ley federal impide que los inmigrantes no autorizados se inscriban en planes del Mercado del ACA, reciban Creditos Fiscales de Prima o se unan al Medicaid estandar, con una excepcion federal importante: el Medicaid de emergencia, que reembolsa a los hospitales por estabilizar a cualquier paciente independientemente del estatus migratorio bajo EMTALA. Mas alla de ese piso, la cobertura en 2026 varia de casi universal (Oregon) a solo emergencias (la mayoria de los estados), dependiendo enteramente de donde vive una persona.

introParagraphs.en: Four states have built meaningful coverage programs for undocumented immigrants using state-only or waiver funding: California (Medi-Cal), New York (state-funded Medicaid for seniors), Illinois (now limited to seniors and emergencies after the July 2025 program cuts), and Oregon (OHP Healthier Oregon, the most inclusive in the country). Mixed-status families have one additional consideration: U.S.-born children in an undocumented household ARE eligible for Medicaid and CHIP on their own merits, and applying for children does not trigger public charge consequences for the parents. The public charge rule in 2026 remains under active regulatory revision; as of May 2026 the DHS 2022 framework still governs, and Medicaid use by most covered categories does not count against a public charge determination.
introParagraphs.es: Cuatro estados han creado programas de cobertura significativos para inmigrantes indocumentados utilizando fondos exclusivos del estado o de exenciones: California (Medi-Cal), Nueva York (Medicaid financiado por el estado para mayores), Illinois (ahora limitado a mayores y emergencias tras los recortes del programa de julio de 2025) y Oregon (OHP Healthier Oregon, el mas inclusivo del pais). Las familias de estatus mixto tienen una consideracion adicional: los ninos nacidos en EE.UU. en un hogar indocumentado SON elegibles para Medicaid y CHIP por sus propios meritos, y solicitar cobertura para los ninos no activa consecuencias de carga publica para los padres.

en: CA only: new enrollment frozen; renewals allowed
es: Adultos indocumentados inscritos antes del 1 ene 2026
Solo CA: nueva inscripcion congelada; renovaciones permitidas

en: All ages, income at or below 138% FPL ($22,025/yr single)
OR only, most inclusive state program in 2026
es: Plan de Salud de Oregon (OHP): Healthier Oregon
Todas las edades, ingresos hasta 138% FPL ($22,025/ano individual)
Solo OR, programa estatal mas inclusivo en 2026

en: New York state-funded Medicaid for seniors 65+
Undocumented immigrants age 65 and older, income at or below 138% FPL
NY only: covers seniors, not working-age adults
es: Medicaid financiado por NY para mayores 65+
Inmigrantes indocumentados de 65 anos o mas, ingresos hasta 138% FPL
Solo NY: cubre mayores, no adultos en edad laboral

en: All 50 states: stabilization only, not ongoing care
es: Emergencias que amenazan la vida para personas con ingresos elegibles
Los 50 estados: solo estabilizacion, no atencion continua

en: Primary care, preventive care, sliding-scale cost regardless of status
es: Atencion primaria, preventiva, costo a escala variable sin importar el estatus
Aprox. 1,400 organizaciones de centros de salud en todo el pais

footnote.en: ACA Marketplace plans and Premium Tax Credits are not available to undocumented immigrants regardless of income. Children born in the U.S. qualify for Medicaid and CHIP on their own and should be enrolled separately.
footnote.es: Los planes del Mercado del ACA y los Creditos Fiscales de Prima no estan disponibles para inmigrantes indocumentados independientemente de sus ingresos. Los ninos nacidos en EE.UU. califican para Medicaid y CHIP por cuenta propia y deben inscribirse por separado.

optionsOverview.source: DHCS California, Oregon Health Authority, NY State of Health, HRSA FQHC Finder, Medicaid.gov

heading.en: Option 1: California Medi-Cal (Existing Enrollees Only as of 2026)
heading.es: Opcion 1: Medi-Cal de California (solo inscritos existentes a partir de 2026)

paragraphs.en: California Medi-Cal was the most expansive state health coverage program for undocumented immigrants in the U.S. before January 1, 2026. From 2016 through 2022, California extended Medi-Cal to undocumented children, then to adults under 26, then to adults over 50, and finally to all undocumented adults regardless of age beginning January 1, 2024. That full-scope enrollment froze on January 1, 2026, as part of the 2025-26 California state budget. Undocumented adults who were enrolled in full-scope Medi-Cal before the freeze may keep their coverage as long as they complete their annual renewal. New undocumented adult applicants can no longer enroll in full-scope Medi-Cal as of that date.
paragraphs.es: Medi-Cal de California era el programa de cobertura de salud estatal mas amplio para inmigrantes indocumentados en EE.UU. antes del 1 de enero de 2026. Desde 2016 hasta 2022, California extendio Medi-Cal a ninos indocumentados, luego a adultos menores de 26 anos, despues a adultos mayores de 50 y finalmente a todos los adultos indocumentados sin importar la edad a partir del 1 de enero de 2024. Esa inscripcion de cobertura completa se congelo el 1 de enero de 2026 como parte del presupuesto estatal 2025-26 de California. Los adultos indocumentados que estaban inscritos en Medi-Cal de cobertura completa antes del congelamiento pueden mantener su cobertura siempre que completen su renovacion anual. Los nuevos solicitantes adultos indocumentados ya no pueden inscribirse en Medi-Cal de cobertura completa desde esa fecha.

paragraphs.en: Additional 2026 changes for undocumented enrollees in Medi-Cal: routine dental benefits end for adult Medi-Cal members without satisfactory immigration status starting July 1, 2026 (emergency dental remains covered). Starting July 1, 2027, members ages 19 to 59 without satisfactory immigration status will owe a $30 per month premium to keep full-scope coverage; failure to pay drops coverage to emergency and pregnancy services only. Pregnant undocumented immigrants and undocumented children ages 0 to 18 remain eligible for full-scope Medi-Cal without the freeze applying to them. Apply or renew through BenefitsCal at benefits.ca.gov or call 1-800-541-5555. Income eligibility follows standard Medi-Cal at 138% FPL (income at or below $22,025 for a single person in 2026).
paragraphs.es: Cambios adicionales en 2026 para los inscritos indocumentados en Medi-Cal: los beneficios dentales de rutina terminan para los miembros adultos de Medi-Cal sin estatus migratorio satisfactorio a partir del 1 de julio de 2026 (la atencion dental de emergencia permanece cubierta). A partir del 1 de julio de 2027, los miembros de 19 a 59 anos sin estatus migratorio satisfactorio deberan pagar $30 al mes para mantener la cobertura completa; si no pagan, la cobertura se reduce a servicios de emergencia y embarazo solamente. Las inmigrantes indocumentadas embarazadas y los ninos indocumentados de 0 a 18 anos siguen siendo elegibles para Medi-Cal de cobertura completa sin que el congelamiento les aplique. Solicite o renueve en BenefitsCal en benefits.ca.gov o llame al 1-800-541-5555.

heading.en: Option 2: Oregon Health Plan (OHP): Healthier Oregon, All Ages and Statuses
heading.es: Opcion 2: Plan de Salud de Oregon (OHP): Healthier Oregon, todas las edades y estatus

paragraphs.en: Oregon's Healthier Oregon program, which took full effect on July 1, 2023, is the most inclusive state health coverage program for undocumented immigrants in the United States as of 2026. Undocumented immigrants of any age who live in Oregon and whose household income is at or below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level ($22,025 for a single person, $45,540 for a household of four in 2026) can enroll in full Oregon Health Plan benefits through the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). Unlike California, Oregon did not impose a 2026 enrollment freeze, and no premium is required at or below the income threshold. Coverage includes primary care, specialist visits, behavioral health, dental, vision, prescription drugs, and long-term support services. OHP does not count as a public charge factor and does not affect immigration status.
paragraphs.es: El programa Healthier Oregon, que entro en plena vigencia el 1 de julio de 2023, es el programa de cobertura de salud estatal mas inclusivo para inmigrantes indocumentados en los Estados Unidos en 2026. Los inmigrantes indocumentados de cualquier edad que viven en Oregon y cuyo ingreso familiar es igual o inferior al 138% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza ($22,025 para una persona, $45,540 para un hogar de cuatro en 2026) pueden inscribirse en los beneficios completos del Plan de Salud de Oregon a traves del OHP. A diferencia de California, Oregon no impuso un congelamiento de inscripcion en 2026, y no se requiere prima en el umbral de ingresos o por debajo de el. La cobertura incluye atencion primaria, visitas a especialistas, salud conductual, dental, vision, medicamentos recetados y servicios de apoyo a largo plazo.

paragraphs.en: Undocumented immigrants in Oregon apply through the Oregon ONE system at one.oregon.gov or by calling OHP Customer Service at 1-800-699-9075. Spanish-language enrollment assistance is available. Children in families earning above 138% FPL may qualify for OHP through the children's expanded threshold (up to 305% FPL for children). Enrollment in OHP is year-round; no special enrollment period is required because Medicaid has no open enrollment window. Oregon's program covers non-citizens regardless of whether they are DACA recipients, asylum seekers, Temporary Protected Status holders, or fully undocumented individuals.
paragraphs.es: Los inmigrantes indocumentados en Oregon solicitan a traves del sistema Oregon ONE en one.oregon.gov o llamando al Servicio al Cliente del OHP al 1-800-699-9075. Hay asistencia para la inscripcion en espanol disponible. Los ninos en familias que ganan mas del 138% FPL pueden calificar para el OHP a traves del umbral expandido para ninos (hasta el 305% FPL para ninos). La inscripcion en el OHP es durante todo el ano; no se requiere un periodo de inscripcion especial porque el Medicaid no tiene ventana de inscripcion abierta.

heading.en: Option 3: New York State-Funded Medicaid for Undocumented Seniors (65+)
heading.es: Opcion 3: Medicaid financiado por NY para mayores indocumentados (65+)

paragraphs.en: New York State extended full-scope Medicaid to undocumented immigrants age 65 and older effective January 1, 2024, using state funds outside the federal Medicaid matching structure. In 2026 this program continues and is not subject to the federal H.R.1 Medicaid cuts because it is state-funded. Undocumented seniors in New York who meet standard Medicaid income thresholds (at or below 138% FPL, which is $22,025 for a single person and $45,540 for a household of four in 2026) can enroll in mainstream Medicaid managed care plans covering preventive care, primary care, specialist visits, prescriptions, and long-term care services including home care. Undocumented pregnant individuals in New York continue to be eligible for full Medicaid coverage under a separate pregnancy-coverage rule. For working-age undocumented adults in New York (ages 19 to 64 and not pregnant), the main fallback remains Emergency Medicaid plus community health centers.
paragraphs.es: Nueva York extendio el Medicaid de cobertura completa a los inmigrantes indocumentados de 65 anos o mas efectivo el 1 de enero de 2024, utilizando fondos estatales fuera de la estructura federal de compensacion del Medicaid. En 2026, este programa continua y no esta sujeto a los recortes federales del Medicaid de H.R.1 porque es financiado por el estado. Los mayores indocumentados en Nueva York que cumplen los umbrales de ingresos estandar del Medicaid (igual o inferior al 138% FPL, que es $22,025 para una persona y $45,540 para un hogar de cuatro en 2026) pueden inscribirse en planes de atencion gestionada del Medicaid convencional. Los inmigrantes indocumentados embarazados en Nueva York siguen siendo elegibles para la cobertura completa del Medicaid.

paragraphs.en: Undocumented seniors in New York apply through their local Department of Social Services or, in New York City, through the NYC Human Resources Administration. NY State of Health (the state marketplace at nystateofhealth.ny.gov) provides application assistance for this population. For the working-age undocumented population in New York who are not pregnant and under 65, the New York City Health and Hospitals network (NYC Health + Hospitals) runs MetroPlus and NYC Care, which provide subsidized primary care to low-income uninsured residents regardless of immigration status and are not subject to federal immigration status rules.
paragraphs.es: Los mayores indocumentados en Nueva York solicitan a traves de su Departamento local de Servicios Sociales o, en la ciudad de Nueva York, a traves de la Administracion de Recursos Humanos de Nueva York. NY State of Health (el mercado estatal en nystateofhealth.ny.gov) proporciona asistencia para la solicitud. Para la poblacion indocumentada en edad laboral en Nueva York que no esta embarazada y tiene menos de 65 anos, la red NYC Health + Hospitals ofrece atencion primaria subsidiada a residentes no asegurados de bajos ingresos independientemente del estatus migratorio.

heading.en: Option 4: Emergency Medicaid and EMTALA Rights (Nationwide)
heading.es: Opcion 4: Medicaid de emergencia y derechos EMTALA (a nivel nacional)

paragraphs.en: Emergency Medicaid is a federal program that reimburses hospitals for the cost of stabilizing patients who meet all standard Medicaid eligibility criteria except immigration status. In all 50 states, an income-eligible undocumented immigrant who presents with a life-threatening emergency condition will receive hospital stabilization covered under Emergency Medicaid. The Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act (EMTALA) separately requires every Medicare-participating hospital with an emergency department to screen and stabilize any patient with an emergency medical condition regardless of immigration status or ability to pay. EMTALA applies to all 6,100-plus emergency departments in the country and cannot be waived by hospital policy.
paragraphs.es: El Medicaid de emergencia es un programa federal que reembolsa a los hospitales por el costo de estabilizar a los pacientes que cumplen todos los criterios estandar de elegibilidad del Medicaid excepto el estatus migratorio. En los 50 estados, un inmigrante indocumentado con ingresos elegibles que llega con una condicion de emergencia que amenaza la vida recibira estabilizacion hospitalaria cubierta bajo el Medicaid de emergencia. La Ley de Tratamiento Medico de Emergencia y Trabajo (EMTALA) requiere por separado que cada hospital con departamento de emergencias que participe en Medicare examine y estabilice a cualquier paciente con una condicion medica de emergencia independientemente del estatus migratorio.

paragraphs.en: Emergency Medicaid covers only the acute stabilization episode, not ongoing care, specialist follow-up, or prescription drugs. Once stabilized and discharged, an undocumented immigrant has no federally guaranteed continued coverage. Starting October 1, 2026, H.R.1 (the 2025 reconciliation law) reduces the federal matching payment (FMAP) for Emergency Medicaid from 90% to 50% for expanded-Medicaid-eligible individuals, shifting more cost to states. This may lead some states to narrow Emergency Medicaid scope after October 2026, but all states must maintain at minimum the EMTALA-required stabilization mandate regardless of matching payment levels. Applying for Emergency Medicaid does not trigger a public charge consequence and does not expose any household member to immigration enforcement.
paragraphs.es: El Medicaid de emergencia cubre solo el episodio de estabilizacion aguda, no la atencion continua, seguimiento con especialistas o medicamentos recetados. Una vez estabilizado y dado de alta, un inmigrante indocumentado no tiene cobertura continua garantizada a nivel federal. A partir del 1 de octubre de 2026, H.R.1 reduce el pago de compensacion federal (FMAP) para el Medicaid de emergencia del 90% al 50% para las personas elegibles para Medicaid expandido, trasladando mas costos a los estados.

heading.en: Option 5: Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs): Sliding Scale Nationwide
heading.es: Opcion 5: Centros de Salud Calificados Federalmente (FQHCs): Escala variable a nivel nacional

paragraphs.en: Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) are community health organizations funded under Section 330 of the Public Health Service Act to serve medically underserved areas and populations. Approximately 1,400 FQHC organizations operate at nearly 15,000 locations across all 50 states and U.S. territories. FQHCs are required to offer a sliding fee scale based on income and cannot turn away patients due to inability to pay. Many FQHCs serve large immigrant populations and provide care regardless of immigration status. In 2026, undocumented immigrants who cannot access state Medicaid programs can use FQHCs for primary care, preventive screenings, prenatal care, behavioral health, and dental services at costs based on household income.
paragraphs.es: Los Centros de Salud Calificados Federalmente (FQHCs) son organizaciones de salud comunitaria financiadas bajo la Seccion 330 de la Ley de Servicio de Salud Publica para servir a areas y poblaciones medicamente desatendidas. Aproximadamente 1,400 organizaciones FQHC operan en casi 15,000 ubicaciones en los 50 estados y territorios de EE.UU. Los FQHCs deben ofrecer una escala de tarifas basada en ingresos y no pueden rechazar pacientes por incapacidad de pago. Muchos FQHCs sirven a grandes poblaciones inmigrantes y brindan atencion independientemente del estatus migratorio.

paragraphs.en: Note: Federal policy changes in 2025-2026 created ambiguity about whether Section 330 grant funds can be used for non-qualified alien patients in non-emergency, non-immunization contexts. The HRSA guidance from 2025 clarifies that emergency medical care, immunizations, and communicable disease testing and treatment remain covered for all patients under Section 330 funding, while routine primary care for undocumented individuals may be funded through non-federal sources at many FQHCs. Contact your local FQHC directly to confirm current service availability. Use the HRSA Find a Health Center tool at findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov to locate the nearest FQHC. FQHCs in California, New York, Illinois, and Oregon have historically served the largest undocumented immigrant populations and have alternative funding arrangements to fill gaps.
paragraphs.es: Nota: Los cambios en la politica federal de 2025-2026 crearon ambiguedad sobre si los fondos de la subvencion de la Seccion 330 pueden utilizarse para pacientes no calificados en contextos no urgentes y no relacionados con inmunizacion. La guia de HRSA de 2025 aclara que la atencion medica de emergencia, las inmunizaciones y las pruebas y el tratamiento de enfermedades transmisibles siguen cubiertos para todos los pacientes. Contacte a su FQHC local directamente para confirmar la disponibilidad actual de servicios. Use la herramienta HRSA Find a Health Center en findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov para ubicar el centro mas cercano.

intro.en: Undocumented immigrants, immigrants without papers, and mixed-status families are targeted by products and misinformation that can cause serious financial and legal harm. Know these pitfalls before purchasing any coverage:
intro.es: Los inmigrantes indocumentados y las familias de estatus mixto son blanco de productos y desinformacion que pueden causar danos financieros y legales graves. Conozca estas trampas antes de comprar cualquier cobertura:

en: Applying for ACA Marketplace plans or Premium Tax Credits

Undocumented immigrants are categorically ineligible. Submitting a marketplace application while undocumented can expose personal information and may constitute a fraudulent application, creating legal risk. Only lawfully present immigrants and U.S. citizens may use the marketplace.
es: Solicitar planes del Mercado del ACA o Creditos Fiscales de Prima

Los inmigrantes indocumentados son categoricamente inelegibles. Presentar una solicitud del mercado siendo indocumentado puede exponer informacion personal y puede constituir una solicitud fraudulenta, creando riesgo legal. Solo los inmigrantes presentes legalmente y los ciudadanos estadounidenses pueden usar el mercado.

en: Health share ministries and short-term plans marketed as ACA alternatives

Health share ministries (Medi-Share, Liberty HealthShare, Samaritan) are NOT insurance. They have no legal obligation to pay claims and exclude pre-existing conditions. Short-term plans do not cover pre-existing conditions and can rescind coverage. Both are inappropriate as a primary coverage source for anyone, especially for immigrants unfamiliar with U.S. insurance practices.
es: Ministerios de compartir salud y planes a corto plazo comercializados como alternativas al ACA

Los ministerios de compartir salud (Medi-Share, Liberty, Samaritan) NO son seguros. No tienen obligacion legal de pagar reclamaciones y excluyen condiciones preexistentes. Los planes a corto plazo no cubren condiciones preexistentes y pueden cancelar la cobertura. Ambos son inapropiados como fuente de cobertura principal.

en: Assuming public charge risk from Medicaid use

Emergency Medicaid, OHP, Medi-Cal, and state-funded Medicaid programs for undocumented immigrants are generally not counted as public charge factors under the 2022 DHS framework that governs in 2026. Medicaid used by children under 21 and pregnant individuals does not count. Do not forgo needed care based on unfounded fear of public charge consequences. Consult an immigration attorney if uncertain.
es: Asumir riesgo de carga publica por el uso del Medicaid

El Medicaid de emergencia, OHP, Medi-Cal y los programas estatales de Medicaid para inmigrantes indocumentados generalmente no se cuentan como factores de carga publica bajo el marco DHS de 2022 que rige en 2026. El Medicaid utilizado por ninos menores de 21 anos y personas embarazadas no cuenta. No renuncie a la atencion necesaria por temor infundado.

en: Missing Medi-Cal renewals as an existing California enrollee

If you were enrolled in Medi-Cal before January 1, 2026, missing your annual renewal will terminate your coverage and you cannot re-enroll. Set reminders; renew through BenefitsCal (benefits.ca.gov) the moment you receive the renewal notice. The state mails renewal packets 90 to 60 days before the renewal deadline.
es: Perderse las renovaciones de Medi-Cal como inscrito existente en California

Si estaba inscrito en Medi-Cal antes del 1 de enero de 2026, perderse su renovacion anual terminara su cobertura y no podra volver a inscribirse. Configure recordatorios; renueve a traves de BenefitsCal (benefits.ca.gov) en cuanto reciba el aviso de renovacion. El estado envia paquetes de renovacion entre 90 y 60 dias antes del plazo de renovacion.

en: Assuming children in mixed-status households cannot get Medicaid

U.S.-born children of undocumented parents are U.S. citizens and qualify for Medicaid and CHIP based on the household's income, not the parents' immigration status. Applying for a child does not create public charge risk for the parent and does not expose the parent to immigration enforcement. Every U.S.-born child in an income-eligible household should be enrolled.
es: Asumir que los ninos en hogares de estatus mixto no pueden obtener Medicaid

Los ninos nacidos en EE.UU. de padres indocumentados son ciudadanos estadounidenses y califican para Medicaid y CHIP segun los ingresos del hogar, no el estatus migratorio de los padres. Solicitar cobertura para un nino no crea riesgo de carga publica para el padre ni lo expone a la aplicacion de inmigración. Cada nino nacido en EE.UU. en un hogar con ingresos elegibles debe estar inscrito.

footnote.en: Verify any coverage option directly with the state agency or FQHC. Rules changed significantly in 2025-2026 across all four featured states. Never rely solely on broker representations about what a plan covers.
footnote.es: Verifique cualquier opcion de cobertura directamente con la agencia estatal o el FQHC. Las reglas cambiaron significativamente en 2025-2026 en los cuatro estados destacados. Nunca dependa unicamente de las representaciones de un agente sobre lo que cubre un plan.

traps.source: DHCS CA, Oregon Health Authority, NY Health Access, NILC, HRSA, Medicaid.gov

heading.en: State programs for undocumented immigrants in 2026: CA, NY, IL, and OR compared
heading.es: Programas estatales para inmigrantes indocumentados en 2026: CA, NY, IL y OR comparados

paragraphs.en: No two states took the same path in 2026 on coverage for undocumented immigrants and mixed-status households. California's Medi-Cal reached the widest coverage in 2024 but froze new enrollment for adults on January 1, 2026, under budget pressure estimated to save $3.3 billion annually by 2028-29. Oregon's OHP Healthier Oregon program, by contrast, added no new restrictions in 2026 and remains fully open to all ages and immigration statuses at or below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level. New York built a state-funded Medicaid program for undocumented seniors age 65 and older starting January 1, 2024, which continues in 2026. Illinois eliminated its Health Benefits for Immigrant Adults (HBIA) program for ages 42 to 64 effective July 1, 2025, due to state budget constraints, leaving Emergency Medicaid and FQHCs as the main options for working-age undocumented adults in Illinois.
paragraphs.es: Ninguno de los dos estados siguio el mismo camino en 2026 en cuanto a la cobertura para inmigrantes indocumentados y hogares de estatus mixto. California llego a la cobertura mas amplia en 2024 pero congelo nuevas inscripciones para adultos el 1 de enero de 2026 bajo presion presupuestaria estimada en $3.3 mil millones de ahorro anual para 2028-29. El programa OHP Healthier Oregon de Oregon, en contraste, no agrego nuevas restricciones en 2026 y permanece completamente abierto a todas las edades y estatus migratorios hasta el 138% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza. Nueva York construyo un programa de Medicaid financiado por el estado para mayores indocumentados de 65 anos o mas a partir del 1 de enero de 2024, que continua en 2026. Illinois elimino su programa de Beneficios de Salud para Adultos Inmigrantes (HBIA) para edades de 42 a 64 anos efectivo el 1 de julio de 2025.

caption.en: State coverage programs for undocumented immigrants: 2026 status
caption.es: Programas de cobertura estatal para inmigrantes indocumentados: estado en 2026

en: HBIS (65+) for existing enrollees only; Emergency Medicaid available
es: HBIS (65+) solo para inscritos existentes; Medicaid de emergencia disponible

footnote.en: All four programs are state-funded to varying degrees and are not subject to federal ACA Marketplace rules. Emergency Medicaid remains available in all 50 states for income-eligible individuals regardless of the state program status shown above.
footnote.es: Los cuatro programas son financiados por el estado en distintos grados y no estan sujetos a las reglas del Mercado del ACA federal. El Medicaid de emergencia sigue disponible en los 50 estados para personas con ingresos elegibles independientemente del estado del programa estatal indicado arriba.

table.source: DHCS CA, Oregon Health Authority, NY Health Access, Illinois HFS, NILC 2026

heading.en: Income eligibility lookup for Oregon OHP and state Medicaid programs in 2026
heading.es: Consulta de elegibilidad por ingresos para OHP de Oregon y programas estatales de Medicaid en 2026

paragraphs.en: Oregon's OHP Healthier Oregon and the New York state-funded Medicaid for seniors both use 138% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) as the income threshold, the same as standard ACA Medicaid expansion. The 2026 FPL guidelines (published by HHS ASPE in January 2026) set the per-person increment at $5,680 for the 48 contiguous states and D.C. The table below shows the 138% FPL threshold by household size for 2026. Undocumented immigrants applying for OHP or the New York senior program must have household income at or below the applicable threshold. Income includes wages, self-employment earnings, rental income, and most government payments; it excludes child support received, foster care payments, and Supplemental Security Income.
paragraphs.es: OHP Healthier Oregon de Oregon y el Medicaid financiado por el estado de Nueva York para mayores utilizan ambos el 138% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza (FPL) como umbral de ingresos, igual que la expansion estandar del Medicaid del ACA. Las directrices FPL de 2026 (publicadas por HHS ASPE en enero de 2026) establecen el incremento por persona en $5,680 para los 48 estados contiguos y D.C. La tabla a continuacion muestra el umbral del 138% FPL por tamano del hogar para 2026.

caption.en: 138% FPL income thresholds by household size: 2026 (48 states + D.C.)
caption.es: Umbrales de ingresos del 138% FPL por tamano del hogar: 2026 (48 estados + D.C.)

footnote.en: Alaska and Hawaii have higher FPL thresholds. Oregon OHP also uses 138% FPL for adults; children in Oregon qualify at up to 305% FPL. These thresholds apply to state Medicaid programs; ACA Premium Tax Credits are not available to undocumented immigrants regardless of income.
footnote.es: Alaska y Hawaii tienen umbrales FPL mas altos. El OHP de Oregon tambien usa el 138% FPL para adultos; los ninos en Oregon califican hasta el 305% FPL. Estos umbrales aplican a los programas estatales de Medicaid; los Creditos Fiscales de Prima del ACA no estan disponibles para inmigrantes indocumentados independientemente de los ingresos.

table.source: HHS ASPE 2026 Poverty Guidelines, Oregon OHA, Medicaid.gov

heading.en: No access to ACA plans or Premium Tax Credits: what that means for undocumented immigrants in 2026
heading.es: Sin acceso a planes del ACA ni Creditos Fiscales de Prima: lo que eso significa para inmigrantes indocumentados en 2026

paragraphs.en: Federal law (ACA Section 1312(f)(3)) bars undocumented immigrants from purchasing health coverage through ACA Marketplace plans and from receiving the Premium Tax Credit (PTC). This bar applies even if the person's income would otherwise qualify for a subsidy. The 2026 return of the subsidy cliff (enhanced PTCs from ARPA and the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026) makes ACA Marketplace plans more expensive for citizens and lawfully present immigrants, but that pricing shift is irrelevant for undocumented immigrants because they cannot access the marketplace at all, regardless of income level. Form 7206 (the self-employed health insurance deduction) is also not applicable to undocumented immigrants because they are not permitted to deduct premiums against income reported on federal tax returns in the context of marketplace plans, and they cannot purchase the qualifying plans that Form 7206 supports. Health Savings Accounts (HSA) paired with High-Deductible Health Plans (HDHP) are similarly inaccessible because HSA-eligible HDHP plans are purchased on the ACA Marketplace or through employer sponsors, neither of which is available to undocumented immigrants. Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) are employer-only and not available to undocumented immigrants who are typically not on formal W-2 payroll.
paragraphs.es: La ley federal (Seccion 1312(f)(3) del ACA) impide que los inmigrantes indocumentados compren cobertura de salud a traves de los planes del Mercado del ACA y reciban el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC). Esta prohibicion aplica incluso si los ingresos de la persona de otro modo calificarian para un subsidio. El retorno del abismo de subsidio en 2026 (los PTCs mejorados de ARPA y la Ley de Reduccion de Inflacion expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026) hace que los planes del Mercado del ACA sean mas caros para ciudadanos e inmigrantes presentes legalmente, pero ese cambio de precios es irrelevante para los inmigrantes indocumentados porque no pueden acceder al mercado en absoluto, independientemente del nivel de ingresos. El Formulario 7206 (la deduccion de seguro medico para trabajadores independientes) tampoco aplica a los inmigrantes indocumentados en este contexto. Las Cuentas de Ahorros para la Salud (HSA) con Planes de Deducible Alto (HDHP) son igualmente inaccesibles. Las Cuentas de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) son exclusivas del empleador y no estan disponibles para inmigrantes indocumentados.

paragraphs.en: Mixed-status families where some members ARE lawfully present (U.S.-born children, DACA recipients with valid status, TPS holders, lawful permanent residents) face a split situation. Lawfully present members of the household can purchase ACA Marketplace plans and may qualify for Premium Tax Credits based on household MAGI. The undocumented members of the same household cannot. For 2026 PTC calculation purposes, the household income of all members is counted, but only lawfully present members' coverage needs are included in the tax credit computation. Section 1095-A, the IRS form that marketplace enrollees receive and use to reconcile premium tax credits on Form 8962, applies only to lawfully present family members who enrolled through the marketplace.
paragraphs.es: Las familias de estatus mixto donde algunos miembros SI estan presentes legalmente (ninos nacidos en EE.UU., beneficiarios de DACA con estatus valido, titulares de TPS, residentes permanentes legales) enfrentan una situacion dividida. Los miembros del hogar presentes legalmente pueden comprar planes del Mercado del ACA y pueden calificar para Creditos Fiscales de Prima basados en el MAGI del hogar. Los miembros indocumentados del mismo hogar no pueden. La Seccion 1095-A, el formulario del IRS que los inscritos en el mercado reciben y usan para conciliar los creditos fiscales de prima en el Formulario 8962, aplica solo a los miembros del hogar presentes legalmente que se inscribieron a traves del mercado.

heading.en: HSA and FSA access for undocumented immigrants in 2026
heading.es: Acceso a HSA y FSA para inmigrantes indocumentados en 2026

paragraphs.en: Health Savings Accounts (HSA) are not available to undocumented immigrants as a practical matter in 2026. HSA eligibility requires enrollment in an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP), defined by the IRS as a plan with a minimum deductible of $1,700 for self-only coverage or $3,400 for family coverage in 2026 (per IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19). HSA-qualified HDHPs are purchased through the ACA Marketplace or through employer-sponsored group plans. Because undocumented immigrants cannot access either the ACA Marketplace or most formal employer-sponsored plans, the HSA pathway is inaccessible for most undocumented immigrants in practice. The 2026 HSA contribution limits ($4,400 self-only / $8,750 family / $1,000 catch-up for ages 55 and older) and the triple tax advantage (tax-deductible contribution, tax-free growth, tax-free qualified medical withdrawals) that make HSAs valuable for other populations are therefore not available to undocumented immigrants.
paragraphs.es: Las Cuentas de Ahorros para la Salud (HSA) no estan disponibles para inmigrantes indocumentados como cuestion practica en 2026. La elegibilidad para el HSA requiere inscripcion en un Plan de Deducible Alto (HDHP) calificado para HSA, definido por el IRS como un plan con un deducible minimo de $1,700 para cobertura individual o $3,400 para cobertura familiar en 2026 (segun IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19). Los HDHP calificados para HSA se compran a traves del Mercado del ACA o a traves de planes de grupo patrocinados por el empleador. Debido a que los inmigrantes indocumentados no pueden acceder a ninguno de estos, la via del HSA es inaccesible. Los limites de contribucion al HSA de 2026 ($4,400 individual / $8,750 familiar / $1,000 adicional para mayores de 55 anos) y la ventaja fiscal triple que hacen valiosas las HSA para otras poblaciones no estan disponibles para los inmigrantes indocumentados.

paragraphs.en: Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) are employer-only benefits available through formal W-2 employment. Undocumented immigrants who work in the informal economy or in cash-wage arrangements outside formal payroll typically have no access to FSA accounts. Even undocumented immigrants in formal W-2 employment may have restricted access depending on employer plan offerings. The distinction between HSA and FSA is important: FSA is employer-administered and use-it-or-lose-it; HSA is individually owned and portable. Neither is accessible through the state programs (Medi-Cal, OHP, New York state Medicaid) that serve undocumented immigrants, as those are state Medicaid programs that do not offer HSA or FSA pairing options.
paragraphs.es: Las Cuentas de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) son beneficios exclusivos del empleador disponibles a traves del empleo formal W-2. Los inmigrantes indocumentados que trabajan en la economia informal o en acuerdos de salario en efectivo fuera de la nomina formal tipicamente no tienen acceso a las cuentas FSA. La distincion entre HSA y FSA es importante: la FSA es administrada por el empleador y es de usar o perder; la HSA es propiedad individual y portable. Ninguna es accesible a traves de los programas estatales (Medi-Cal, OHP, Medicaid de NY) que sirven a los inmigrantes indocumentados.

heading.en: Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) triggers for undocumented immigrants in 2026
heading.es: Activadores del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) del Mercado para inmigrantes indocumentados en 2026

paragraphs.en: The ACA Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) rules under 45 CFR 155.420 do not apply to undocumented immigrants because undocumented individuals cannot enroll in ACA Marketplace plans at any time. For state Medicaid programs that cover undocumented immigrants (Medi-Cal, OHP, New York state Medicaid for seniors), enrollment is year-round with no SEP or open enrollment window required. This is one meaningful advantage of state Medicaid coverage over marketplace coverage: there is no enrollment period. An undocumented immigrant who becomes income-eligible for Oregon OHP can apply any day of the year. An undocumented immigrant in California who was enrolled before the 2026 freeze and then lost coverage due to a missed renewal can potentially seek assistance through the county social services office.
paragraphs.es: Las reglas del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) del Mercado del ACA bajo 45 CFR 155.420 no aplican a los inmigrantes indocumentados porque las personas indocumentadas no pueden inscribirse en planes del Mercado del ACA en ningun momento. Para los programas estatales de Medicaid que cubren a los inmigrantes indocumentados (Medi-Cal, OHP, Medicaid estatal de Nueva York para mayores), la inscripcion es durante todo el ano sin necesidad de un SEP ni una ventana de inscripcion abierta. Esta es una ventaja significativa de la cobertura del Medicaid estatal sobre la cobertura del mercado: no hay periodo de inscripcion.

paragraphs.en: For mixed-status families with lawfully present members who CAN use the ACA Marketplace, standard SEP triggers apply to those family members: loss of other coverage (60-day window), marriage or divorce, having or adopting a child, moving to a new state or coverage area, income change crossing the Medicaid threshold, DACA or other immigration status change granting lawful presence (which itself triggers a 60-day SEP), and aging off a parent's plan at 26. A DACA recipient whose status is renewed mid-year has a 60-day SEP from the renewal date to enroll in a marketplace plan. If a mixed-status family member gains lawful presence through a visa or status change, they trigger a 60-day SEP on the ACA Marketplace. Undocumented members of the same household do not gain marketplace eligibility from the status change of a relative.
paragraphs.es: Para las familias de estatus mixto con miembros presentes legalmente que SI pueden usar el Mercado del ACA, los activadores estandar del SEP aplican a esos miembros de la familia: perdida de otra cobertura (ventana de 60 dias), matrimonio o divorcio, tener o adoptar un hijo, mudarse a un nuevo estado o area de cobertura, cambio de ingresos que cruza el umbral del Medicaid, cambio de estatus migratorio que otorga presencia legal (lo que en si mismo activa un SEP de 60 dias) y cumplir 26 anos en el plan de los padres. Un beneficiario de DACA cuyo estatus se renueva a mitad de ano tiene un SEP de 60 dias desde la fecha de renovacion para inscribirse en un plan del mercado.

list.en: Loss of other coverage (state Medicaid termination, job loss): 60-day SEP for lawfully present family members
list.es: Perdida de otra cobertura (terminacion del Medicaid estatal, perdida de empleo): SEP de 60 dias para miembros del hogar presentes legalmente
Matrimonio o divorcio: SEP de 60 dias

list.en: Birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child: 60-day SEP (child is U.S. born and eligible for Medicaid/CHIP immediately)
list.es: Nacimiento, adopcion o colocacion en acogida de un nino: SEP de 60 dias (el nino nace en EE.UU. y es elegible para Medicaid/CHIP de inmediato)

list.en: DACA status renewal or new grant of lawful presence: 60-day SEP for that individual
list.es: Renovacion del estatus DACA o nueva concesion de presencia legal: SEP de 60 dias para ese individuo

list.en: Permanent move to a state with different Medicaid rules (e.g., moving to Oregon, which covers undocumented immigrants): apply for OHP year-round without an SEP
list.es: Mudanza permanente a un estado con diferentes reglas de Medicaid (por ejemplo, mudarse a Oregon, que cubre inmigrantes indocumentados): solicitar OHP durante todo el ano sin un SEP

list.en: Income change that crosses the OHP or state Medicaid income threshold: apply year-round for state programs
list.es: Cambio de ingresos que cruza el umbral de ingresos de OHP o Medicaid estatal: solicitar programas estatales durante todo el ano

heading.en: How to apply for state programs in 2026: step-by-step
heading.es: Como solicitar programas estatales en 2026: paso a paso

paragraphs.en: Enrollment for state Medicaid programs covering undocumented immigrants is year-round, but the application process varies by state. Oregon OHP: apply at one.oregon.gov or call 1-800-699-9075. California Medi-Cal (existing enrollees renewing): renew at benefits.ca.gov or call 1-800-541-5555; county social services offices also accept in-person renewals. New York state-funded senior Medicaid: apply at the local Department of Social Services or NYC Human Resources Administration; assistance available at nystateofhealth.ny.gov. FQHCs: locate the nearest center at findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov and contact directly for intake.
paragraphs.es: La inscripcion para los programas estatales de Medicaid que cubren inmigrantes indocumentados es durante todo el ano, pero el proceso de solicitud varia segun el estado. OHP de Oregon: solicite en one.oregon.gov o llame al 1-800-699-9075. Medi-Cal de California (inscritos existentes renovando): renueve en benefits.ca.gov o llame al 1-800-541-5555. Medicaid estatal para mayores en Nueva York: solicite en el Departamento local de Servicios Sociales o en la Administracion de Recursos Humanos de NYC. FQHCs: localice el centro mas cercano en findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov.

list.en: Step 1: Determine state eligibility. Check whether your state has a program for undocumented immigrants (Oregon: all ages; California: existing enrollees 19+ and all children 0-18; New York: seniors 65+ and pregnant individuals; Illinois: Emergency Medicaid and FQHCs only).
list.es: Paso 1: Determine la elegibilidad estatal. Verifique si su estado tiene un programa para inmigrantes indocumentados (Oregon: todas las edades; California: inscritos existentes 19+ y todos los ninos 0-18; Nueva York: mayores 65+ y personas embarazadas; Illinois: solo Medicaid de emergencia y FQHCs).

list.en: Step 2: Gather documents. You do not need a Social Security number or immigration document to apply for state-funded Medicaid in Oregon, California, or New York. You will need proof of state residency (utility bill, lease, bank statement), proof of identity (foreign passport, consular ID such as the Matricula Consular, or birth certificate), and proof of household income (pay stubs, employer letter, or self-employment records).
list.es: Paso 2: Reuna documentos. No necesita un numero de Seguro Social ni documento migratorio para solicitar el Medicaid financiado por el estado en Oregon, California o Nueva York. Necesitara comprobante de residencia estatal, comprobante de identidad (pasaporte extranjero, ID consular como la Matricula Consular o acta de nacimiento) y comprobante de ingresos del hogar.

list.en: Step 3: Submit the application. Oregon: online at one.oregon.gov. California renewals: online at benefits.ca.gov or at the county social services office. New York: at the local Department of Social Services. FQHCs: contact the health center directly; most have patient navigators who assist with enrollment.
list.es: Paso 3: Envie la solicitud. Oregon: en linea en one.oregon.gov. Renovaciones en California: en linea en benefits.ca.gov o en la oficina de servicios sociales del condado. Nueva York: en el Departamento local de Servicios Sociales. FQHCs: contacte al centro de salud directamente; la mayoria tiene navegadores de pacientes que ayudan con la inscripcion.

list.en: Step 4: Await eligibility determination. Oregon determines eligibility within 45 days for non-disability applications. California Medi-Cal renewals are processed within 90 days. New York county agencies typically decide within 45 days.
list.es: Paso 4: Espere la determinacion de elegibilidad. Oregon determina la elegibilidad dentro de los 45 dias para solicitudes que no sean por discapacidad. Las renovaciones de Medi-Cal en California se procesan dentro de los 90 dias. Las agencias del condado de Nueva York tipicamente deciden dentro de los 45 dias.

list.en: Step 5: Common reasons applications are denied. Income above 138% FPL (verify with the household-size table above). Residency not established in the state (must prove current state residence). Missing identity documentation (bring a consular ID, foreign passport, or equivalent). For California Medi-Cal: enrollment after January 1, 2026, as a new adult applicant. For New York senior Medicaid: age below 65.
list.es: Paso 5: Razones comunes por las que se deniegan las solicitudes. Ingresos por encima del 138% FPL (verifique con la tabla de tamano del hogar arriba). Residencia no establecida en el estado (debe demostrar residencia actual en el estado). Documentacion de identidad faltante (traiga una ID consular, pasaporte extranjero o equivalente). Para Medi-Cal de California: inscripcion despues del 1 de enero de 2026 como nuevo solicitante adulto. Para el Medicaid para mayores en Nueva York: edad menor de 65 anos.

en.question: What is the cheapest health insurance option for undocumented immigrants in 2026?
en.answer: The cheapest option depends on the state. In Oregon, income-eligible undocumented immigrants of any age can enroll in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) at no cost (premium-free at 138% FPL and below, which is $22,025 annually for a single person in 2026). In California, undocumented adults who were enrolled in Medi-Cal before January 1, 2026, can continue coverage free of charge through annual renewals. In New York, undocumented seniors age 65 and older who are income-eligible can enroll in state-funded Medicaid at no premium cost. In states without dedicated programs, Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) provide sliding-scale primary care often for $20 to $40 per visit based on income, and Emergency Medicaid covers life-threatening emergencies at no direct patient cost. ACA Marketplace plans and Premium Tax Credits are not available to undocumented immigrants.

en.question: Do undocumented immigrants qualify for the Premium Tax Credit or ACA subsidies?
en.answer: No. Federal law (ACA Section 1312(f)(3)) categorically bars undocumented immigrants from purchasing ACA Marketplace plans or receiving the Premium Tax Credit (PTC). This applies regardless of income level. The 2026 return of the subsidy cliff (enhanced PTCs from ARPA and the Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026) affects citizens and lawfully present immigrants, not undocumented individuals who were never eligible for those credits. Mixed-status families with lawfully present members (U.S.-born children, DACA recipients, TPS holders, lawful permanent residents) can access marketplace plans and PTCs for those eligible members; the undocumented members of the household cannot.

en.question: Can undocumented immigrants deduct health insurance premiums on taxes?
en.answer: Form 7206, the self-employed health insurance deduction, does not apply to undocumented immigrants in the standard sense, because it requires purchasing a qualifying plan (ACA Marketplace or employer-sponsored HDHP), which are not available to undocumented immigrants. Undocumented immigrants who file U.S. tax returns using an Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) can potentially deduct unreimbursed medical expenses above 7.5% of AGI on Schedule A, the itemized deduction, the same medical expense deduction available to any taxpayer. This deduction does not reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE; it reduces income tax only. The Form 7206 deduction specifically and the associated HSA tax benefits do not apply to undocumented immigrants because the qualifying plans that enable those deductions are not accessible to them.

en.question: Can undocumented immigrants use an HSA in 2026?
en.answer: Not as a practical matter. Health Savings Accounts (HSA) require enrollment in an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) with a minimum deductible of $1,700 self-only or $3,400 family in 2026, per IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19. HSA-qualified HDHPs are purchased through the ACA Marketplace or through formal employer-sponsored plans. Since undocumented immigrants cannot access either, the HSA pathway and its 2026 contribution limits ($4,400 self-only / $8,750 family) and triple tax advantage are unavailable. State Medicaid programs like Oregon OHP and California Medi-Cal that serve undocumented immigrants are not HSA-paired HDHP plans. Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) are employer-only and similarly unavailable to most undocumented immigrants.

en.question: Does applying for Medicaid or a state program create a public charge problem?
en.answer: As of May 2026, using Emergency Medicaid, Oregon OHP, California Medi-Cal, or New York state-funded Medicaid generally does not count against a public charge determination under the DHS 2022 framework that remains in effect. DHS published a proposed rule in November 2025 that could broaden public charge consideration, but that proposal is still in rulemaking and the 2022 framework governs currently. Medicaid received by children under 21 and pregnant individuals explicitly does not count under any current rule. Consult a qualified immigration attorney before making coverage decisions if you have an active immigration case or pending visa petition. Immigrants without papers who need emergency care should not forgo treatment due to unfounded public charge fears. Do not forgo needed care.

en.question: When can an undocumented immigrant enroll in a state Medicaid program outside a set enrollment period?
en.answer: State Medicaid programs (Oregon OHP, California Medi-Cal, New York state senior Medicaid) have year-round enrollment. Unlike ACA Marketplace plans, which require a Special Enrollment Period (SEP) outside open enrollment, Medicaid has no enrollment window. An income-eligible undocumented immigrant can apply for Oregon OHP or New York senior Medicaid on any day of the year. In California, existing Medi-Cal enrollees can renew year-round; new adult applicants cannot enroll as of January 1, 2026. For FQHCs, walk-in and scheduled appointments are available year-round regardless of any enrollment period. Emergency Medicaid is triggered automatically by an emergency event and does not require advance enrollment.

en.question: What coverage is available to undocumented immigrants in Illinois after the HBIA program ended?
en.answer: Illinois eliminated the Health Benefits for Immigrant Adults (HBIA) program for ages 42 to 64 effective July 1, 2025. As of 2026, undocumented adults in Illinois have access to: (1) Emergency Medicaid for life-threatening emergency stabilization, covered statewide under federal mandate; (2) FQHCs (Federally Qualified Health Centers) that provide sliding-scale primary care regardless of immigration status; (3) free and charitable clinics serving uninsured and underinsured populations; and (4) the Health Benefits for Immigrant Seniors (HBIS) program, which remains for existing enrollees age 65 and older. No new HBIS enrollment is being accepted. Undocumented children age 0 to 18 in Illinois may still qualify through All Kids or CHIP on their own merits. Undocumented pregnant individuals qualify for emergency pregnancy-related Medicaid.

en.question: Are U.S.-born children of undocumented parents eligible for Medicaid and CHIP?
en.answer: Yes. U.S.-born children of undocumented parents are U.S. citizens and are fully eligible for Medicaid and CHIP based on the household's income, regardless of the parents' immigration status. In 2026, children in families at or below 138% FPL typically qualify for Medicaid; those between 138% and 200-300% FPL (varies by state) qualify for CHIP. Applying for a child's Medicaid or CHIP coverage does not trigger public charge consequences for the parent and does not expose the parent to immigration enforcement. Every income-eligible U.S.-born child in an undocumented or mixed-status household should be enrolled in Medicaid or CHIP. Apply through your state's Medicaid agency or at healthcare.gov for CHIP in states using the federal marketplace.

es.question: Cual es la opcion de seguro medico mas barata para inmigrantes indocumentados en 2026?
es.answer: La opcion mas barata depende del estado. En Oregon, los inmigrantes indocumentados con ingresos elegibles de cualquier edad pueden inscribirse en el Plan de Salud de Oregon (OHP) sin costo (sin prima hasta el 138% FPL, que es $22,025 anuales para una persona en 2026). En California, los adultos indocumentados inscritos en Medi-Cal antes del 1 de enero de 2026 pueden continuar la cobertura de forma gratuita mediante renovaciones anuales. En Nueva York, los mayores indocumentados de 65 anos o mas con ingresos elegibles pueden inscribirse en el Medicaid financiado por el estado sin costo de prima. En estados sin programas dedicados, los FQHCs ofrecen atencion primaria a escala variable a menudo por $20 a $40 por visita segun los ingresos, y el Medicaid de emergencia cubre emergencias que amenazan la vida. Los planes del Mercado del ACA y los Creditos Fiscales de Prima no estan disponibles para inmigrantes indocumentados.

es.question: Los inmigrantes indocumentados califican para el Credito Fiscal de Prima o los subsidios del ACA?
es.answer: No. La ley federal (Seccion 1312(f)(3) del ACA) prohibe categoricamente a los inmigrantes indocumentados comprar planes del Mercado del ACA o recibir el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC). Esto aplica independientemente del nivel de ingresos. Las familias de estatus mixto con miembros presentes legalmente (ninos nacidos en EE.UU., beneficiarios de DACA, titulares de TPS, residentes permanentes legales) pueden acceder a los planes del mercado y PTCs para esos miembros elegibles; los miembros indocumentados del hogar no pueden.

es.question: Los inmigrantes indocumentados pueden deducir las primas del seguro medico en los impuestos?
es.answer: El Formulario 7206 (la deduccion del seguro medico para trabajadores independientes) no aplica a los inmigrantes indocumentados en el sentido estandar, porque requiere comprar un plan calificado (Mercado del ACA o HDHP patrocinado por el empleador), que no estan disponibles para los inmigrantes indocumentados. Los inmigrantes indocumentados que presentan declaraciones de impuestos en EE.UU. usando un Numero de Identificacion del Contribuyente Individual (ITIN) pueden deducir los gastos medicos no reembolsados por encima del 7.5% del AGI en el Anexo A (deduccion detallada). Esta deduccion no reduce el impuesto al trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE; reduce el impuesto sobre la renta solamente.

es.question: Los inmigrantes indocumentados pueden usar una HSA en 2026?
es.answer: No como cuestion practica. Las Cuentas de Ahorros para la Salud (HSA) requieren inscripcion en un HDHP calificado para HSA con un deducible minimo de $1,700 individual o $3,400 familiar en 2026, segun IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19. Los HDHP calificados para HSA se compran a traves del Mercado del ACA o a traves de planes formales patrocinados por el empleador. Dado que los inmigrantes indocumentados no pueden acceder a ninguno de estos, la via del HSA y sus limites de contribucion de 2026 ($4,400 individual / $8,750 familiar) y la ventaja fiscal triple no estan disponibles. Las Cuentas de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) son exclusivas del empleador y similarmente no estan disponibles para la mayoria de los inmigrantes indocumentados.

es.question: Solicitar el Medicaid o un programa estatal crea un problema de carga publica?
es.answer: A partir de mayo de 2026, el uso del Medicaid de emergencia, OHP de Oregon, Medi-Cal de California o el Medicaid financiado por el estado de Nueva York generalmente no cuenta en una determinacion de carga publica bajo el marco DHS de 2022 que sigue en vigor. El Medicaid recibido por ninos menores de 21 anos y personas embarazadas explicitamente no cuenta bajo ninguna regla actual. Consulte a un abogado de inmigracion calificado antes de tomar decisiones de cobertura si tiene un caso de inmigracion activo o una peticion de visa pendiente.

es.question: Cuando puede inscribirse un inmigrante indocumentado en un programa estatal de Medicaid?
es.answer: Los programas estatales de Medicaid (OHP de Oregon, Medi-Cal de California, Medicaid estatal para mayores de Nueva York) tienen inscripcion durante todo el ano. A diferencia de los planes del Mercado del ACA, que requieren un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (SEP) fuera de la inscripcion abierta, el Medicaid no tiene ventana de inscripcion. Un inmigrante indocumentado con ingresos elegibles puede solicitar el OHP de Oregon o el Medicaid para mayores de Nueva York cualquier dia del ano. En California, los inscritos existentes en Medi-Cal pueden renovar durante todo el ano; los nuevos solicitantes adultos no pueden inscribirse desde el 1 de enero de 2026.

es.question: Que cobertura esta disponible para inmigrantes indocumentados en Illinois despues de que termino el programa HBIA?
es.answer: Illinois elimino el programa de Beneficios de Salud para Adultos Inmigrantes (HBIA) para edades de 42 a 64 anos efectivo el 1 de julio de 2025. A partir de 2026, los adultos indocumentados en Illinois tienen acceso a: (1) Medicaid de emergencia para la estabilizacion de emergencias que amenazan la vida; (2) FQHCs que brindan atencion primaria a escala variable independientemente del estatus migratorio; (3) clinicas gratuitas y de caridad; y (4) el programa HBIS (para mayores de 65 anos) solo para inscritos existentes. Los ninos indocumentados de 0 a 18 anos en Illinois pueden calificar a traves de AllKids o CHIP por sus propios meritos.

es.question: Los ninos nacidos en EE.UU. de padres indocumentados son elegibles para Medicaid y CHIP?
es.answer: Si. Los ninos nacidos en EE.UU. de padres indocumentados son ciudadanos estadounidenses y son completamente elegibles para Medicaid y CHIP segun los ingresos del hogar, independientemente del estatus migratorio de los padres. Solicitar la cobertura de Medicaid o CHIP de un nino no activa consecuencias de carga publica para el padre y no expone al padre a la aplicacion de inmigración. Cada nino con ingresos elegibles nacido en EE.UU. en un hogar indocumentado o de estatus mixto debe estar inscrito en Medicaid o CHIP. Solicite a traves de la agencia de Medicaid de su estado o en healthcare.gov para CHIP en los estados que usan el mercado federal.
